user_task: Nemo Retriever library setup on K8s GPU machine: Extraction, Embedding, Reranking NIMs helm charts downloaded and deployed. (On request, the agent should validate GPU, Kubernetes, and Helm prerequisites, ask before installing missing K8s/Helm, then deploy NRL via Helm.)
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, SETUP bucket, row 3. No seed queries were provided for this row in the spec — the prompts below are synthesized from the user-task wording and the defining behaviors (validate GPU+K8s+Helm prereqs; ASK before installing missing K8s/Helm; deploy the extraction/embedding/reranking NIMs via Helm; then point the CLI at the in-cluster NIM endpoints for a smoke ingest+query).

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — (1) clean library install, (2) deploy the Extraction/Embedding/Reranking NIMs helm charts on a GPU node and confirm pods/services healthy (<= 30 min), (3) ingest 1 PDF against the in-cluster NIM endpoints, (4) retrieve a grounded query, all binary pass/fail
success_criteria.time: Helm deploy of the NIMs on a GPU node <= 30 min; full end-to-end (clean -> deploy -> ingest -> cited query) <= 40 min
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'deploy NeMo Retriever on my GPU Kubernetes cluster with Helm' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must (a) validate prereqs and ASK before installing missing k8s/helm (no auto-install), (b) deploy the NIMs via `helm install/upgrade` of the nemo_retriever chart, and (c) run `retriever ingest` then `retriever query` with the in-cluster NIM invoke-urls (NOT hosted ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com defaults)

environment.gpu: present on at least one schedulable K8s node (nvidia.com/gpu allocatable >= 1; NVIDIA device plugin / GPU Operator installed)
environment.kubernetes: a reachable cluster (kubectl context set; `kubectl version` / `kubectl get nodes` succeed). Chart requires kubeVersion >= 1.25.0.
environment.nim_operator: The NIM templates are gated on the apps.nvidia.com/v1alpha1 API group (NVIDIA NIM Operator). If absent, NIMCache/NIMService templates short-circuit and the chart degrades to 'external NIM URLs only' mode. Operator install (when missing, AFTER asking the user): `helm install k8s-nim-operator nvidia/k8s-nim-operator --namespace nim-operator --create-namespace`.
environment.ask_before_install: If kubectl/helm/the NIM Operator/the GPU device-plugin is MISSING, the agent must REPORT what is missing and ASK for confirmation before installing anything. It must NOT silently install Kubernetes/Helm/operator components.

chart.name: nemo-retriever
chart.deploy_command_template: helm upgrade --install retriever ./nemo_retriever/helm -n <ns> --create-namespace --set ngcImagePullSecret.create=true --set ngcImagePullSecret.password=$NGC_API_KEY --set ngcApiSecret.create=true --set ngcApiSecret.password=$NGC_API_KEY
chart.reranker_nim: rerankqa is OPTIONAL and disabled by default (not auto-wired). Enable with --set nimOperator.rerankqa.enabled=true -> Service llama-nemotron-rerank-vl-1b-v2, port 8000. The row explicitly requires the Reranking NIM, so this suite turns it on.
verify_commands: kubectl get nodes -o wide   # confirm a GPU node (nvidia.com/gpu allocatable)
kubectl get pods -n <ns> -w   # wait for NIMService + service pods Ready
kubectl port-forward svc/retriever 7670:7670 -n <ns>   # then curl http://localhost:7670/v1/health

in_cluster_nim_endpoints.note: These DNS names resolve in-cluster. When driving the CLI from outside the cluster, port-forward each Service (or use the retriever-service HTTP API) and substitute http://localhost:<port>. The CLI itself talks to these via --page-elements-invoke-url / --ocr-invoke-url / --table-structure-invoke-url / --embed-invoke-url / --reranker-invoke-url.

cases.title: Prerequisite validation only: detect GPU + Kubernetes + Helm, report readiness, ASK before installing anything missing
cases.complexity_axis: Baseline: validate the three prerequisites the row names (GPU on a K8s node, a reachable Kubernetes cluster, Helm 3) and report a readiness verdict. The DEFINING discipline of this row — if something (kubectl/helm/NIM Operator/GPU device-plugin) is missing the agent must ASK before installing, never auto-install. No deploy and no ingest yet.
cases.prompt: I want to run NeMo Retriever on my GPU Kubernetes cluster. Before doing anything, check that I'm actually ready: do I have a GPU node, a working kubectl/cluster, and Helm? Tell me what's present and what's missing — and if something needs installing, ask me first, don't just install it.
expected_commands: kubectl get nodes -o wide   # at least one Ready node
kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.allocatable.nvidia\.com/gpu}'   # >= 1 GPU allocatable (device plugin / GPU Operator present)
kubectl api-resources | grep nimservices   # is the NVIDIA NIM Operator (apps.nvidia.com/v1alpha1) installed?

expected_output.readiness_report: A per-prerequisite verdict: GPU node (present/absent), Kubernetes (reachable, version >= 1.25), Helm 3 (present), NIM Operator CRDs (present/absent).
expected_output.ask_discipline: If any of kubectl/helm/NIM Operator/GPU device-plugin is MISSING, the agent states what it would install (e.g. NIM Operator via `helm install k8s-nim-operator nvidia/k8s-nim-operator --namespace nim-operator --create-namespace`) and ASKS for confirmation rather than running it.
expected_output.no_deploy: No `helm install` of the retriever chart and no `retriever ingest`/`query` are run in this case.

pass_when: agent checks all three prereqs: a GPU node, a reachable Kubernetes cluster, and Helm
agent reports a clear present/missing verdict per prereq
if anything is missing, the agent ASKS before installing — it does NOT auto-install kubectl/helm/the NIM Operator
no retriever chart deploy and no ingest/query in this rung

cases.title: Helm deploy of the Extraction/Embedding/Reranking NIMs to a GPU node + verify pods/services healthy
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the deploy step the row is named for: after prereqs pass, `helm upgrade --install` the nemo_retriever chart so the four core NIMs (page-elements, table-structure, OCR, VLM embed) plus the opt-in reranker NIM land as NIMCache+NIMService on the GPU node, then verify pods/services are Ready. Still no ingest/query — the deliverable here is a healthy deployment within the <= 30 min Helm SLA.
cases.prompt: Prereqs are good. Deploy the NeMo Retriever NIMs to my GPU cluster with Helm — I need extraction, embedding, AND reranking — into namespace 'nrl'. Then show me the pods and services are healthy before we load any documents.
expected_commands: helm upgrade --install retriever ./nemo_retriever/helm -n nrl --create-namespace --set ngcImagePullSecret.create=true --set ngcImagePullSecret.password=$NGC_API_KEY --set ngcApiSecret.create=true --set ngcApiSecret.password=$NGC_API_KEY --set nimOperator.rerankqa.enabled=true
kubectl get pods -n nrl -w   # wait for NIMService pods + retriever-service pod Ready

expected_output.helm_release: Release 'retriever' deployed in namespace nrl (chart nemo-retriever 26.5.0).
expected_output.nims_reconciled: NIMService objects created and Ready for the 3 model classes the row requires: extraction (nemotron-page-elements-v3, nemotron-table-structure-v1, nemotron-ocr-v1), embedding (llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2), reranking (llama-nemotron-rerank-vl-1b-v2). First-time NIMCache reconciliation downloads weights to a PVC — allow several minutes.
expected_output.services_up: ClusterIP Services exist for each NIM (port 8000) and for the retriever service (port 7670); health check `curl http://localhost:7670/v1/health` via port-forward returns OK.
expected_output.sla: Helm deploy to all-pods-Ready within <= 30 min.

pass_when: agent runs `helm upgrade --install` of the nemo_retriever chart (not a hand-rolled kubectl apply)
all three NIM classes deploy: extraction (page-elements + table-structure + OCR), embedding (VLM embed), and reranking (rerankqa enabled)
NIMService + retriever-service pods reach Ready and Services are present
deploy completes within the 30-minute Helm SLA

cases.title: Smoke ingest of 1 PDF against the in-cluster NIM endpoints
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the first real CLI work: ingest a single PDF, with the CLI pointed at the IN-CLUSTER NIM service URLs (not the hosted ai.api.nvidia.com defaults). Proves the deployed extraction + embedding NIMs are reachable and write rows to LanceDB. No query yet.
cases.prompt: Now that the NIMs are running in the cluster, load data/woods_frost.pdf through them — use the in-cluster extraction and embedding endpoints, not NVIDIA's cloud — and confirm it wrote rows.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.endpoint_assertion: Extraction + embedding hit the in-cluster Service DNS names (nemotron-*:8000 / llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2:8000), NOT ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com.
expected_output.note: From outside the cluster the agent may port-forward each NIM Service and substitute http://localhost:<port>; the requirement is that the URLs are the deployed in-cluster NIMs, not the hosted defaults.

pass_when: single `retriever ingest` runs against woods_frost.pdf and writes rows to lancedb/nemo-retriever
the extraction + embedding invoke-urls point at the in-cluster NIM Services (not hosted ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com)
ingest summary line reports 1 file ingested

cases.title: Retrieve a grounded query against the in-cluster embed + rerank NIMs
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the query leg and exercises the deployed RERANKING NIM (the third model class the row requires). Ingest a table PDF via the in-cluster extraction+embed NIMs, then query with the in-cluster embed AND reranker endpoints to surface a specific table cell. This is the first rung that closes ingest -> query end to end against the cluster.
cases.prompt: Using the NIMs deployed in my cluster (including the reranker), load data/table_test.pdf and then tell me — reranked, best match first — what value James had in 2019.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "James value in 2019" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table --embed-invoke-url http://llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2:8000/v1/embeddings --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2 --reranker-invoke-url http://llama-nemotron-rerank-vl-1b-v2:8000 --reranker-model-name llama-nemotron-rerank-vl-1b-v2 --rerank

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.rerank_assertion: --reranker-invoke-url points at the in-cluster llama-nemotron-rerank-vl-1b-v2 Service; reranked order != dense-only order; no hosted reranker call
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; page_number 1-indexed

pass_when: ingest + query round-trip uses the in-cluster embed AND reranker NIM endpoints (not hosted defaults)
query returns the correct cell value (978) citing table_test.pdf p1
--rerank is on and routed to the deployed reranker NIM
if --content-types is used it is text,table

cases.title: Full operational-pass acceptance: clean -> ask-before-install -> Helm deploy (<=30 min) -> ingest -> cited query, end-to-end <= 40 min
cases.complexity_axis: Composed acceptance gate for the row: a clean library install, prereq validation WITH the ask-before-installing discipline honored, a Helm deploy of all three NIM classes onto the GPU node within 30 min, ingest of a folder into a custom-named index against the in-cluster NIMs, and a cited query routed through the in-cluster embed+rerank NIMs — all end-to-end within 40 minutes, with zero hosted egress.
cases.prompt: Do a clean end-to-end setup on my GPU Kubernetes cluster. First validate GPU/Kubernetes/Helm and, if anything is missing, ask me before installing it. Then deploy the extraction, embedding, and reranking NIMs with Helm into namespace 'nrl', load data/ into a fresh index called 'k8s_smoke' through the in-cluster NIMs, and answer with a citation: in which poem does the speaker say they have 'miles to go before I sleep'? Confirm the deploy finished under 30 minutes, the whole thing under 40, and that nothing called NVIDIA's cloud endpoints.
expected_commands: kubectl get nodes -o wide ; helm version   # validate; ASK before installing anything missing

helm upgrade --install retriever ./nemo_retriever/helm -n nrl --create-namespace --set ngcImagePullSecret.create=true --set ngcImagePullSecret.password=$NGC_API_KEY --set ngcApiSecret.create=true --set ngcApiSecret.password=$NGC_API_KEY --set nimOperator.rerankqa.enabled=true
kubectl get pods -n nrl -w   # wait Ready (<= 30 min)

$RETRIEVER ingest data/ --lancedb-uri lancedb --table-name k8s_smoke --page-elements-invoke-url http://nemotron-page-elements-v3:8000/v1/infer --ocr-invoke-url http://nemotron-ocr-v1:8000/v1/infer --table-structure-invoke-url http://nemotron-table-structure-v1:8000/v1/infer --embed-invoke-url http://llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2:8000/v1/embeddings --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2

$RETRIEVER query "miles to go before I sleep" --table-name k8s_smoke --top-k 5 --embed-invoke-url http://llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2:8000/v1/embeddings --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2 --reranker-invoke-url http://llama-nemotron-rerank-vl-1b-v2:8000 --reranker-model-name llama-nemotron-rerank-vl-1b-v2 --rerank

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/k8s_smoke. (branch_summary pdf:3)
expected_output.answer: 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' by Robert Frost; cite woods_frost.pdf p1
assertions: prereqs validated and, if anything was missing, the agent ASKED before installing (no silent install of k8s/helm/operator)
helm deploy of all three NIM classes (extraction + embedding + reranking) reached Ready <= 30 min
query reads the SAME table (k8s_smoke) the ingest wrote — not the default nemo-retriever
every returned hit carries non-null source + page_number
ingest + query routed through the in-cluster NIM Services; zero calls to ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com / build.nvidia.com
wall clock (clean state -> cited answer) <= 40 min

pass_when: prereq validation done AND ask-before-installing discipline honored
Helm deploy of extraction+embedding+reranking NIMs Ready <= 30 min
ingest writes lancedb/k8s_smoke and query reads k8s_smoke (table names aligned)
answer correct + citation (woods_frost.pdf p1) present
all CLI work routed through in-cluster NIMs; no hosted egress; end-to-end <= 40 min
cases.known_caveat: The Reranking NIM is OPTIONAL in the chart (nimOperator.rerankqa disabled by default, not auto-wired). Because this row explicitly names the Reranking NIM, the deploy must pass --set nimOperator.rerankqa.enabled=true; a default install would deploy only extraction + embedding and the reranking clause would fail. Also: NIM templates require the NVIDIA NIM Operator (apps.nvidia.com/v1alpha1); without it the chart installs but the NIMs do not reconcile and the service falls back to external NIM URLs — which this acceptance gate is designed to catch.
